title: AES, Block Ciphers, and Primitive Boundaries · Language Lens: Crypto Go Block Permutation
summary: A developer calls AES.Encrypt directly on repeated application records, invents padding, and assumes a strong block cipher alone protects a message. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the block permutation increment in AES primitive boundary and known-answer harness. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evide…
objectives: Explain block-cipher keyed permutation behavior and why messages require an approved mode or higher-level construction.
Apply AES key-size and fixed block-size contracts without confusing key, block, message, and security strength.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Block Permutation · Theory Crypto Go Block Permutation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 1769 during a signed release review. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate AES key and block contracts with known-answer and implementation-boundary evidence; reject toy, obsolete, unauthenticated, self-signed-trust, downgrade, and security-by-obscurity shortcuts; then swap the role, tenant, recipient, algorithm, or context and prove domain separation. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
